title: myrsine subsessilis subsp. subsessilis
intro: Myrsine subsessilis subsp. subsessilis, also known as the New Guinea round-leaf myrtle, is a plant species native to the island of New Guinea. It is a shrub or small tree with medicinal properties, traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues. The plant's leaves, bark, and roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, wounds, and respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's parts are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, or used in traditional medicine practices to promote wound healing and boost the immune system.
